title: Regenerated-cellulose MWCO baseline for selective neuronal-media waste exchange
objective: Identify a passive side-stream dialysis architecture that removes low-molecular-weight waste from neuronal culture medium while preserving protein/growth-factor signals.
hypothesis: Regenerated-cellulose membranes with lower MWCO values will preserve trophic proteins better, while higher MWCO values will clear lactate/ammonium faster but may leak useful signaling molecules. A side-stream design can reveal the useful trade-off without exposing cells to unvalidated cartridge materials.

scope.design_level: screening experiment design, not a wet-lab SOP
scope.cartridge_position: side-stream or external loop, not directly in the cell chamber
scope.first_question: Which MWCO range best balances small-waste clearance and trophic-factor retention?

test_articles.name: Regenerated cellulose, 1 kDa MWCO
test_articles.rationale: Most conservative retention option; expected to preserve trophic proteins but may clear waste slowly.

test_articles.name: Regenerated cellulose, 3.5 kDa MWCO
test_articles.rationale: Common small-molecule dialysis cutoff; expected to retain BDNF/NGF-sized proteins while allowing lactate/ammonium exchange.

test_articles.name: Regenerated cellulose, 10 kDa MWCO
test_articles.rationale: Potential clearance/retention compromise; may preserve BDNF dimer while risking partial NGF or monomeric factor loss.

test_articles.name: Regenerated cellulose, 20 kDa MWCO
test_articles.rationale: Stress-test high-clearance option; useful to quantify whether trophic-factor leakage becomes unacceptable.
test_articles.expected_tradeoff: highest clearance, highest protein-factor leakage risk

test_articles.name: Low-binding PES/SFCA prefilter plus 3.5 kDa RC dialysis
test_articles.rationale: Tests whether debris control can be added before dialysis without stripping proteins.
test_articles.expected_tradeoff: better debris control, added adsorption risk

controls.name: Static conditioned medium, no cartridge
controls.purpose: Measures waste accumulation and factor stability without exchange.

controls.name: Bulk medium exchange reference
controls.purpose: Represents conventional refresh behavior and secretome dilution risk.

controls.name: Fresh medium blank through each module
controls.purpose: Detects extractables, pH/osmolality drift, and baseline adsorption without cell-derived waste.

controls.name: Medium spiked with albumin, BDNF/NGF analog panel, lactate, and ammonium
controls.purpose: Separates material retention/clearance behavior from biological variability.

waste_clearance: optional targeted metabolite panel for unknown inhibitory metabolites
factor_retention: BDNF recovery or proxy neurotrophic protein recovery
NGF recovery or proxy neurotrophic protein recovery
biological_followup: network activity/MEA readout only after media-chemistry gates pass

decision_gates.criterion: Prefer variants that retain high-molecular-weight trophic proteins and albumin while avoiding broad protein loss.
decision_gates.failure_response: If retention is poor, move to lower MWCO or add zwitterionic/low-fouling membrane modification.

decision_gates.criterion: Require measurable lactate and ammonium exchange relative to no-cartridge control.
decision_gates.failure_response: If clearance is weak at acceptable retention, consider larger membrane area, longer side-stream exposure, or a polishing stage.

decision_gates.criterion: Reject variants causing unacceptable pH, osmolality, conductivity, turbidity, or extractables shifts.
decision_gates.failure_response: Change membrane handling, module housing, sterilization workflow, or material family.

decision_gates.criterion: Reject nonselective adsorbers that reduce useful factors even if waste removal is strong.
decision_gates.failure_response: Keep broad adsorbents out of the cell-facing life-support path unless tightly confined and analytically monitored.

risk_controls.risk: MWCO removes helpful small molecules or secretome factors
risk_controls.detection: Compare targeted factor recovery and total protein before/after; add metabolomics in later rounds.

risk_controls.risk: Membrane or housing leachables harm neurons
risk_controls.detection: Run fresh-medium blanks through each module before any biological followup.
Track flow resistance and clearance/retention across repeated or aged-medium exposures.

risk_controls.risk: Bulk exchange outperforms cartridge in short-term tests
risk_controls.detection: Compare against bulk exchange, but evaluate secretome dilution as a separate cost.

next_decisions.condition: 3.5 kDa or 10 kDa RC clears waste and preserves trophic factors
next_decisions.action: Use that MWCO as the passive baseline and test membrane area/flow geometry.

next_decisions.condition: Retention is strong but clearance is weak
next_decisions.action: Increase membrane area or exposure before adding active polishing.

next_decisions.condition: Clearance is strong but trophic-factor loss is high
next_decisions.action: Lower MWCO and prioritize zwitterionic/low-fouling membrane surfaces.

next_decisions.condition: Passive dialysis cannot meet clearance targets
next_decisions.action: Evaluate ICP or confined ion-exchange side-stream module as a second-stage polish.
